name: Grade 1 Music — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

units.title: Investigate: ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to ensemble skills, composing patterns, and connecting music to stories.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
